||while it's more complicated "philosophically" speaking, the Keeper(s) is described by Tawil as Tohu's conscious/conscient existence, so while they *are* Tohu, they're different from their sleeping "body" in that they are...well, fully conscious||
||basically they're tohu when they wake the f up but without destroying the worlds in the process <:pepehaha:416233159454359552> ||

||"Birth of a soul" is probably not meant to be taken as the birth of a soul that didn't exist before (since the Keeper(s) used to be one as Tohu), but more akin to Tohu's soul fragments gaining a more accurately shaped "form" when they dropped onto the world, and truly gaining consciousness in the process
if that makes sense....||
||Keeper also refers to stopping Tohu as "stopping themselves" in chapter 9, so there is an overlap in nature||
||from my under it’s still a birth of a new soul since Keeper is so different and not just a Tohu fragment||

||Yeah
Although for me, the fact that Keeper can act and think for themselves without the need to see themselves as Tohu is already enough proof to say they’re not Tohu and just their own being||
